page: OVHcloud c3-32 is a compute-optimized server instance running on the KVM hypervisor. It features 16 dedicated physical cores and 16 vCPUs powered by an AMD EPYC Milan processor operating at 2.3 GHz. The architecture is x86_64, utilizing a single thread per core configuration. Memory capacity is 32.0 GB, providing 2.0 GB of RAM per core. Local storage consists of a bundled 400 GB NVMe SSD, and the baseline network bandwidth is rated at 2 Gbps, accompanied by one complimentary public IPv4 address.

Benchmark results indicate top-tier single-core CPU performance, with stress-ng single-core testing placing in the top 10% of tested servers. Multi-core CPU performance and PassMark CPU scores fall within the average range. Memory bandwidth for large blocks is strong, ranking in the top 25% for both read and write operations, while small-block cached reads perform at an average level. Text processing and bzip3 decompression also demonstrate strong performance in the top 25% tier.

This instance is configured with a 1:2 vCPU-to-RAM ratio, which prioritizes compute power over memory density. Consequently, it is suited for compute-bound tasks such as software compilation, text processing, and small-scale language model text generation. However, the 32.0 GB memory limit presents a tradeoff for memory-intensive databases or large-scale LLM inference, as evidenced by the lack of performance data for 70B parameter models.
description: The OVHcloud c3-32 is a compute-optimized instance featuring 16 dedicated AMD EPYC Milan physical cores running at 2.3 GHz on a KVM hypervisor. It is equipped with 32.0 GB of RAM, a 400 GB local NVMe SSD, and 2 Gbps baseline network bandwidth. Benchmarks show top-tier single-core CPU performance and strong large-block memory bandwidth, alongside strong bzip3 decompression and text processing capabilities. Conversely, multi-core CPU performance and database operations align with average tiers. With a 1:2 vCPU-to-RAM ratio, this server is configured for compute-intensive workloads such as software compilation, text processing, and small-model text generation, though its moderate memory capacity presents a tradeoff for memory-heavy database deployments.
og_description: OVHcloud c3-32 is a compute-optimized server with 16 dedicated AMD EPYC Milan cores, 32 GB RAM, and a 400 GB NVMe SSD, delivering top-tier single-core CPU performance for compute-bound workloads.
meta_description: OVHcloud c3-32 is a compute-optimized KVM instance with 16 AMD EPYC Milan vCPUs, 32 GB RAM, and a 400 GB NVMe SSD for compute-heavy workloads.
tagline: A dedicated AMD EPYC compute instance featuring top-tier single-core performance, bundled NVMe storage, and a balanced 1:2 memory-to-core ratio.

Bundled 400 GB local NVMe SSD and 2 Gbps baseline network bandwidth
Top-tier single-core CPU performance and strong large-block memory bandwidth
Optimized for software compilation, text processing, and small-model text generation
1:2 vCPU-to-RAM ratio prioritizing compute density over massive memory arrays
